The current account deficit narrowed to 1.2 per cent of GDP in FY2024, supported by robust services exports and steady remittance inflows. 
[Doc 1]
per_query.ground_truth: India's current account deficit (CAD) narrowed to 0.7% of GDP in FY2024, from 2.0% in FY2023. The improvement was driven by lower oil import bills, strong services exports (especially IT and software), and robust remittances of USD 120 billion. RBI assessed the CAD as eminently manageable and well-financed by capital inflows.
per_query.correctness_reason: one sentence

per_query.ground_truth: Neither PhonePe (47% share) nor Google Pay (34% share) was in compliance with the 30% cap as of March 2024. NPCI acknowledged that hard enforcement during the transition period would damage user trust and merchant operations.
